summary: Backend: 26/26 pytest tests passed (100%). Frontend: 11/11 Playwright flows passed including login (CPF & e-mail), signup with masks + auto-login, route guards (CandidateRoute /inscricao and AdminRoute /donaspainel), admin login, admin panel tabs (Cadastros + Inscrições), search filter, delete-with-cascade, and tab switching. The auth + admin panel Fase 1 implementation is solid and fully functional end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins=['*'] is not permitted by the CORS spec and will be rejected by browsers if cookies are ever used. Currently harmless because frontend uses Bearer tokens, but should be tightened before adding cookie-based auth or in production.
Uses deprecated @app.on_event hooks; migrate to lifespan context manager.

Self-healing block re-seeds/updates the admin every login attempt — fine for dev but allows ADMIN_PASSWORD env rotation to overwrite the DB hash silently. Acceptable for now; document in production runbook.

issues: Search input lacks data-testid (had to fall back to placeholder selector)
Trash/delete button row-action lacks an explicit data-testid (used title/aria-label fallback)
Consider adding a confirm dialog with custom modal instead of relying on window.confirm (which we auto-accepted in tests)

action_items: Add data-testid to admin panel search input and per-row delete buttons (e.g., admin-search-input, admin-delete-cadastro-<id>) to make automated regression tests more robust.
Tighten CORS: replace '*' with explicit origins when allow_credentials=True, OR set allow_credentials=False since frontend uses Bearer tokens only.
(Optional) Migrate startup/shutdown to FastAPI lifespan context manager.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is well-structured (375 lines, single-responsibility helpers, async throughout).
_id is properly excluded from every Mongo response (projections + doc.pop) — no ObjectId leakage.
Password hashing uses bcrypt with $2b$ format; no plain-text storage; password_hash projected out of all GETs.
JWT uses HS256 with secret from env and 24h expiry; role claim properly distinguishes candidate vs admin and 403 is returned on role mismatch.
Cascade delete on DELETE /api/admin/cadastros/{id} works as designed (verified by automated test).
Unique indexes on users.cpf and users.email guarantee data integrity even under race conditions; register also pre-checks for friendly 400 message.
test_credentials: Admin donas / Seinao10@@ (works). Candidates created on-the-fly via /api/auth/register; tests clean up TEST_-prefixed users via admin DELETE.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed candidates and remove them via admin delete teardown. No persistent seed left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<public-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. All tests are idempotent and self-cleaning. Admin credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md (donas / Seinao10@@). Frontend uses Bearer tokens in localStorage keys 'candidate_token' and 'admin_token' — clear them via page.evaluate('localStorage.clear()') before testing guards. /inscricao loads a static iframe (data-testid='static-page-inscricao') — only the URL match matters for the guard test.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
